summary: Large ftcpuattack decomp slice for fighter CPU attack selection. It replaced four nonmatching stubs with C for ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, and ftCo_800B77E8, reconstructed local attack/object structs, and adjusted the ftCo_800B5AB0 call signature plus Fighter_x1A88_t::x50 typing. decomp.dev reported seven improvements in main/melee/ft/ftcpuattack, with the new functions reaching roughly 88-94% rather than full match; there was no human PR body or review feedback in the slice.

key_files.role: Primary implementation file; added local structs and C bodies for ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, ftCo_800B77E8, plus matching-oriented edits in ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800BB220.
Updated ftCo_800B5AB0 prototype from two parameters to three: Fighter*, void*, void*.
Changed Fighter_x1A88_t::x50 from int to u32, matching its new use as a raw value passed to ftCo_800B5AB0.

what_changed: Added #include <math_ppc.h> in ftcpuattack.c, apparently to use the PPC inline sqrtf behavior in the new physics/prediction code.
Expanded ftCo_AttackEntry from mostly padding into observed fields: cmd at +0x00, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, weight at +0x18, x1C, and x20.
Added local placeholder structs ftCo_x50_attr and ftCo_x50_t with offset-based fields used by ftCo_800B5AB0.

Implemented ftCo_800B4AB0: filters attack entries by CPU level and blocked commands, predicts relative fighter/target positions, range-tests entries, then uses HSD_Randf and entry weights to choose a command while filling cpu->x6C and cpu->x74 bounds.
Implemented ftCo_800B52AC as a close variant of ftCo_800B4AB0 with an extra reach parameter affecting horizontal/vertical range checks.
Implemented ftCo_800B5AB0 for the cpu->x50-related target object path: it reads ftCo_x50_t/ftCo_x50_attr fields, performs similar prediction/range/weight filtering, and returns a selected command.

Implemented ftCo_800B77E8 as character-specific CPU command exclusion/insertion logic. It appends command IDs into cpu->xCC_array while keeping cpu->xEC below 8 and uses mpCheckAll/ftCo_800A0FB0 probes for several character cases.
Updated ftCo_800B8A9C so the cpu->x50 branch calls ftCo_800B5AB0(fp, (void*) cpu->x50, ((void**) Fighter_804D64FC->x14)[fp->kind]) instead of the old two-argument call.
Adjusted ftCo_800B8A9C literals from 5.0f/1000.0f to 5.0/1000.0 in one ftCo_800A0FB0 call, consistent with matching/sdata2-sensitive tuning.

Refactored ftCo_800BB220 for matching: initialized temp_r31 at declaration, replaced an item-kind if/else with a switch over It_Kind_Star and It_Kind_Unk4, and introduced dx/dy/dz temporaries for projected hit capsule vectors.
smart_moves: The PR first reconstructed the shared ftCo_AttackEntry layout, which let three separate CPU attack-selection functions use the same table semantics instead of opaque byte padding.
Uncertain layouts were kept local and offset-named as ftCo_x50_t/ftCo_x50_attr rather than prematurely promoting weak semantic names into global headers.

The three attack-selection functions preserve a repeated shape: stack array of 32 candidate entries, sentinel list traversal until cmd == 0, level/recent-command filtering, range tests, weighted random selection, and an assert-only impossible fallthrough.

The implementation uses explicit near-zero checks with +/-0.00001f and unsuffixed double constants/f64 casts in prediction math, which is the kind of small source-shape control often needed for Metrowerks PPC matches.
ftCo_800B77E8 deliberately keeps repeated per-character blocks and separate p/L/f/n locals instead of abstracting them, likely preserving stack/register layout for decomp progress.
ftCo_800BB220 shows a common matching tactic: readability-neutral rewrites such as dx/dy/dz temporaries and switch-vs-if changes can move an already-decompiled function closer to the target.
decomp_lessons: For ftcpuattack attack tables, do not treat ftCo_AttackEntry as opaque padding: the diff evidences active fields at +0x04/+0x08/+0x0C/+0x10/+0x14/+0x18/+0x1C/+0x20, with weight at +0x18, x20 used as a CPU level gate, and x1C used in a cpu->x80 modulo check.
cpu->xCC_array and cpu->xEC are central to CPU command suppression/selection; ftCo_800B77E8 repeatedly checks cpu->xEC < 8 before appending command IDs.
The CPU selection code combines fighter position, pos_delta, co_attrs.grav, co_attrs.terminal_vel, facing_dir, x34_scale.y, and GA_Air/GA_Ground to predict whether a move entry can reach a target.
ftCo_800B5AB0 requires two opaque arguments after Fighter*: one for the x50 object/state and one for the per-kind attack table from Fighter_804D64FC->x14.
Changing Fighter_x1A88_t::x50 from signed int to u32 is evidence that this field is still semantically uncertain; future work should avoid over-naming it until more x50 uses are understood.

Automated progress was substantial but partial: decomp.dev reported ftCo_800B77E8 at 88.33%, ftCo_800B4AB0 at 91.73%, ftCo_800B52AC at 91.12%, and ftCo_800B5AB0 at 93.60%, so later PRs may still need cleanup for exact matches.
naming_conventions: Address-based names were retained for unknown common fighter functions: ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, ftCo_800B77E8, ftCo_800B8A9C, ftCo_800BB220.
Placeholder struct and field names use offset notation when semantics are not proven: ftCo_x50_t, ftCo_x50_attr, x40, x44, x4C, x50, xC0, xCC, xC1C, xC20, xC24, xC28.
Known project enum/style names were used where available: FTKIND_MARIO, FTKIND_FOX, FTKIND_FALCO, FTKIND_CAPTAIN, FTKIND_GANON, FTKIND_KOOPA, FTKIND_GKOOPS, GA_Air, GA_Ground, It_Kind_Star.
Stack-local candidate arrays kept stack-offset-style names such as sp3C, sp40, and sp34, useful for matching and search.
Project typedefs f32, f64, s32, u32, bool, Fighter, Item, Vec3, and HitCapsule were used consistently.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use <math_ppc.h> when the target appears to need the Metrowerks/PPC inline sqrtf sequence rather than a generic libm declaration.
Preserve explicit f64 casts and unsuffixed double constants such as 0.5, 1.0, 5.0, and 1000.0 when sdata2/codegen is sensitive.
Manual epsilon checks like value < 0.00001f && value > -0.00001f may match better than abstracting to fabsf or helper calls.
For switch-heavy AI logic, repeated local groups such as u32 fN, int LN, Vec3 nN, Vec3 pN can be necessary to preserve stack layout.
Small expression-shaping changes matter: the ftCo_800BB220 projection code introduced dx/dy/dz locals before assigning sp94/sp60/sp2C.
Weighted selection loops used a pointer variable sel alongside indexed stack-array reads, plus __assert("ftcpuattack.c", line, "0") for unreachable fallthroughs.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The only captured feedback was decomp.dev's GALE01 report showing seven improvements in main/melee/ft/ftcpuattack.
follow_up_queries: ftcpuattack ftCo_800B4AB0 ftCo_800B52AC weighted HSD_Randf attack table matching
ftCo_800B5AB0 cpu->x50 ftCo_x50_t Fighter_804D64FC x14 attack table
ftCo_800B77E8 xCC_array xEC character-specific CPU command suppression
